creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #14)

fp16fml (clang/gcc13 command notation) and
ARMv8.2-FHM/FEAT_FHM (ARM notation) are
apparently called:

asimdfhm

in /proc/cpuinfo output.

And, from what I read, asimdfhm is not listed as
present on the Snapdragon 8cx Gen 3. (But flagm
is listed.) I believe this is consistent with
what FreeBSD is reporting (but it has yet another
notation to interpret).

This appears to mean that the gcc13 cortex-a78c
is the only directly accurate match in clang/gcc.
Clang has fp16fml for a78c (wrong), both clang
and gcc13 have flagm missing for x1c. So,
for compiling, either:

cortex-a78c-fp16fml
or:
cortex-x1c+flagm

for now if one was to tailor the features in some
sort of build.

(I'll note that +pauth and -pauth are rejected on
the command line for c/c++ compiles, at least for
clang. From what I've read it only applies to if
the assembler will accept vs. reject the related
notation.)

For reference for /proc/cpuinfo  ased information
about various soc instruction set features:

https://marcin.juszkiewicz.com.pl/download/tables/arm-socs.html
and its data at:
https://github.com/hrw/arm-socs-table
